mentions.note: Caller Garrett owns ~300 shares; Cramer says hold on to it; new CEO, 4% yield, bouncing off lows for first time in long time; willing to bless owning it, maybe even buy another 100 if it hits $100; wants to have new CEO on show.
Had strong run pre-war, now hitting reversal as consumer caution sets in; big-box retailer exposed to discretionary spending weakness.
New management successfully bringing back old magic; expects repeat of terrific performance from three months ago; comparisons may be over.
32% earnings growth, 5.6% SSS (vs. 2.4% expected), raised full-year outlook; 15x earnings, 3.6% yield; turnaround story headed right direction; home-run quarter.
Rallying on a 4% yield and optimism around the new CEO.
New CEO Mike Fidella must rapidly reinvent after Canadian division exit; lacks Amazon/Walmart/Costco scale; wait for execution results.
Up 6.75% on strong quarter, new CEO welcome; once-worried dividend now looks safe; retailer winner on oil spike.

New CEO Fideli turnaround credible: Q4 margins expanded despite flat comps, non-merchandise revenue soared, $2B investment plan addresses operational failures; trading 15x vs. Walmart 44x, Costco 50x; proof required but early momentum strong.
Up 18% YTD despite Iran war onset; solid start for new CEO Michael Fideli; consumer resilience holding.
Down in rally; retail complex showing weakness.
